pi_items.source: Task description specifies this as the requested database name to be created by the agent
Setup script expects file at /home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip. JSON-LD zip format is documented as recommended format for USLCI import into openLCA (ask.openlca.org)

USLCI database officially contains 'more than 550 process LCIs' (nlr.gov, lcacommons.gov) with some sources citing 660+ processes. Latest versions continue to add processes in quarterly updates (as of 2025)

OpenLCA manual (greendelta.github.io) defines database elements as including all these categories. Processes, Flows, and Flow properties are core elements present in any LCI database including USLCI when imported

privileged_info_summary: The USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database is a real, publicly available dataset from the U.S. Department of Energy containing 550-660+ processes covering fuels, transport, metals, chemicals, and other materials. The import file format (uslci_database.zip in JSON-LD) is correct and recommended by openLCA documentation. The requested database name 'USLCI_Analysis' is a valid task specification. The claimed 'expected_min_processes' of 10 is contradicted by the actual USLCI database which contains over 550 processes. The claimed categories (Processes, Flows, Flow properties) are verified as valid openLCA database elements that will be present in any imported USLCI database.
